findings.evidence: `rg` shows the public declaration at `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/PowMonoidHom.lean:33`; the roadmap names only `index_powMonoidHom_two_le_of_closure` at `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/EffectiveBounds/README.md:29-30,111-113` and the target uses `powMonoidHom 2` at `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/EffectiveBounds/Targets.lean:47-54`.
findings.fix: Make the arbitrary-`n` theorem private or inline it, and expose a square-only `powMonoidHom 2`/`Subgroup.square` bound such as the roadmap’s `index_powMonoidHom_two_le_of_closure`.
findings.issue: `index_powMonoidHom_range_le_of_closure_eq_top` publicly exposes an arbitrary nonzero-power index bound, while the roadmap asks only for the squaring-map abstract lemma.

findings.evidence: `rg` shows the public declaration at `code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/EffectiveBounds/UnitSquares.lean:43`; the Layer 1 target and README ask for the unit-square index only at `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/EffectiveBounds/Targets.lean:47-54` and `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/EffectiveBounds/README.md:29-30`.
findings.fix: Keep the arbitrary-power statement private if needed for the proof, and expose only the square-index theorem required by the roadmap.
findings.issue: `units_pow_index_le` publicly exposes `[O_F^× : (O_F^×)^n] ≤ n^[F:ℚ]` for every nonzero `n`, outside the unit-square-index surface requested here.

summary: The square-index API needed by the roadmap is present, but the PR still exposes arbitrary nonzero-power bounds beyond the named downstream target.
